title: Epigenetic Dynamics Optimization Algorithm and Its Mechanism Study
author: Zhang, Jincheng
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Epigenetics is the study of gene expression regulation and phenotypic shaping. Its core characteristic is the dynamic regulation of gene expression without altering the gene sequence. Key epigenetic mechanisms include DNA methylation, histone modification, noncoding RNA regulation, and chromatin remodeling. These mechanisms are reversible, heritable, multi-layered, dynamic, and environmentally adaptable. Inspired by the principles of epigenetics, this paper proposes a novel heuristic optimization algorithm, Epigenetic Dynamics Optimization (EDO). Building on the core properties of epigenetics, this algorithm incorporates the reversible methylation dynamics mechanism (RMD), the multi-layered histone energy mechanism (MHE), the noncoding RNA cooperative network (NCN), and the chromatin topology remodeling mechanism (CTR), enabling individual activity control, local search optimization, global hopping, and coordinated transfer of neighborhood information. This paper provides a detailed explanation of the algorithmic mechanism, mathematical formula derivation, and optimization strategy design, providing a theoretical foundation for future research on epigenetic heuristic optimization algorithms.
